summarization.a: The figure displays a spectral intensity profile against wavelength, of an O2 plasma in saturation and shortly after a precursor dose.
summarization.b: The figure shows the time-dependent intensity changes of specific molecular emissions (O, H, CO, CN) over time.

data_extraction.a:
| Wavelength (nm) | Intensity (a.u.) O2 plasma |Intensity (a.u.) O2 plasma after ALD cycle |
|---|---|---|
| 360-400 | - | CN range |
| 400-750 | - | CO range |
| 660 | - | H alpha peak |
| 780 | O peak |O peak |

a.question: What type of elements are likely present in the ligands of the used precursor?
a.answer: a	The used plasma is made of oxygen, but the emission also shows H, C and N species, so it it likely that these come from the ligands.

a.question: Is there overlap in peaks for both plasmas?

b.question: Can the conclusion be drawn that there is more CN in the plasma than CO?
b.answer: b	The intensity of the emission of species cannot be compared to others, if the goal is to determine the densities. With OES only the presence of species can be determined.

b.question: What is a safe saturation time for the oxygen plasma step?
b.answer: b	2.5 s would be far enough in saturation.
